author: Arjan Singh, Bhai
title: Qissa Nawan Zamana arthat Rannan de Ghulam
gloss: A short poem which points out in a touching manner the evil effects of wives exercising much influence on their husbands.

author: Balram Das, Sant
title: Guru Purbh Poh Shudi Saptami Siri Guru Gobind Singh ji Maharaj de Autar Dharan di Vadhai Patrika
gloss: Verses in commemoration of the birthday of Guru Gobind Singh.
notes: Guru Gobind Singh birthday (gurpurb) commemorative verses, sponsored by a soldier at Quetta.

author: Barkhurdar, Hafiz
title: Qissa Yusaf Zulaikha
gloss: The well-known love story of the Prophet Joseph and Zulaikha, the Queen of Egypt as told in the Kuran.
notes: Hafiz Barkhurdar's classic Punjabi Yusuf-Zulaikha qissa — Muslim Quranic love-story printed by Sikh booksellers at the Sri Gurmat (Sikh) press.

author: Bhagwan Singh, Bhai
title: Granth Wadda Gur Upkar
gloss: A brief account of the good done by the Sikh Gurus in the cause of religion.

author: Bhagwan Singh, Bhai
title: Naya Qissa Guru Mehma
gloss: Verses in praise of the Sikh Gurus.

author: Deva Singh, Bhai, Granthi
title: Amritsar Sifti da Ghar, Nawan Qissa Dipmala Amritsar
gloss: An account of the illuminations in the Golden Temple of Amritsar on the night of Diwali, a Hindu festival.
notes: Diwali illuminations at the Golden Temple — the registrar's gloss calls Diwali 'a Hindu festival' even for a Sikh-shrine subject; boundary-marking in the cataloguer's own description.

author: Dharm Singh, Bhai, Granthi
title: Guru Upma aur Paintis Akhkhari
gloss: Verses in praise of the Sikh Gurus, with a commentary on the implied meanings of the 35 letters of the Punjabi alphabet.
notes: Paintis Akhari (35-letter Gurmukhi acrostic) devotional form.

author: Gurbakhsh Singh, Bhai
title: Nikiyan Jindan te Wadde Sake
gloss: An account of the martyrdom of the sons of Guru Gobind Singh, the 10th Sikh Guru.
notes: Martyrdom of the Sahibzadas ('small lives, great sacrifices'), sponsored by a Subedar-Major from the Sikh diaspora in Malaya (cf. the Malaya diaspora sponsorship seen in Q3/Q4).
